reasoning: “Valaki megvert valakit” translates to “Someone beat someone.”
“Kit vert meg valaki?” translates to “Who got beaten by someone?”
“Senki nem verte meg a Petyát” translates to “No one beat Peter.”
“Valakit senki nem vert meg” indicates someone was not beaten by anyone.
“Senki nem vert meg mindenkit” translates to “No one beat everyone.”
“Senkit nem vert meg a Petya” translates to “Peter beat no one.”
“Ki nem vert meg senkit?” translates to “Who didn’t beat anyone?”
“Valaki senkit nem vert meg” translates to “Someone didn’t beat anyone.”
“Mindenki megvert valakit” translates to “Everyone beat someone.”
“Valaki megverte a Marcit” translates to “Someone beat Martin.”
“Senkit nem vert meg senki” means no one was beaten, i.e., “No one got beaten.”
“Kit nem vert meg senki?” translates to “Who wasn’t beaten by anyone?”
“Nem vertem meg senkit” translates to “I didn’t beat anyone.”
